text.title: Story Map Structure
text.summary: The four layers of a user story map — backbone, tasks, walking skeleton, and release slices — with rules for building each layer correctly.

rule.statement: The backbone is a left-to-right sequence of 5–10 verb-phrase user activities that represent the user's journey. Use verb phrases ("Create Project"), never nouns ("Project").
rule.rationale: Nouns hide the action and make it impossible to see whether the user can actually accomplish anything. Verb phrases make activities testable: you can verify whether a user can "Create Project."

rule.statement: Under each backbone activity, list specific user tasks ordered vertically by importance (higher = more important). Cap at 6 tasks per column.
rule.rationale: Vertical ordering is the priority signal. More than 6 tasks per column makes the map unreadable and usually means the backbone activity needs to be split.

rule.statement: Include at least one error path or empty-state task per activity — not just the happy path.
rule.rationale: Happy-path-only maps produce releases that break on the first edge case. Error paths are where users abandon products.

rule.statement: The walking skeleton is exactly one task per backbone activity — the minimum set that forms an end-to-end working flow. Every backbone activity must have exactly one skeleton task; the flow must be completeable end-to-end.
rule.rationale: Without the walking skeleton constraint, Release 1 becomes a maximalist feature dump. The skeleton forces the team to ask "what is the thinnest possible flow that works?"

rule.statement: Each release slice is a horizontal cut that spans the full backbone. A release that covers only 2 of 5 activities means users cannot complete the journey.
rule.rationale: Partial-backbone releases ship an incomplete experience. Users who reach the edge of the covered activities are blocked and churn. Every slice must be shippable value — something a user can accomplish end-to-end.

text.reference: Tabular story map with all layers
Validator: skeleton coverage and release-spans-backbone check
